title: Module 1 Project: Course Enrollment Directory
summary: Build a department-to-student enrollment directory through five cumulative multi-table steps.

sketchBlocks.title: Project Overview: Course Enrollment Directory
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown: In this project, you will create a comprehensive course enrollment directory. The goal is to connect various tables in the database to display which students are enrolled in each course and which department offers each course. This involves joining multiple tables: departments, courses, enrollments, and students. By the end of this project, you will have a clear understanding of how to navigate and join tables to produce meaningful reports.

quizDraft.title: Connect Courses to Departments
quizDraft.prompt: Join the courses table to the departments table and return the course title along with the department name.
quizDraft.hint: Use an INNER JOIN to connect courses to departments using the department_id.

help.concept: Joining tables using a foreign key relationship.
help.hint_1: The courses table has a department_id that links to the departments table's id.
help.hint_2: Use SELECT to choose the course title and department name.

quizDraft.title: Add Enrollment Records
quizDraft.prompt: Extend the query to include enrollments so each result row represents one enrollment in a course.
quizDraft.hint: Join the enrollments table to the existing query using the course_id.

help.concept: Extending a join path to include more tables.
help.hint_1: The enrollments table connects to courses via course_id.
help.hint_2: Ensure each row represents a unique enrollment.

quizDraft.starterCode:
SELECT courses.title, departments.name, enrollments.term
FROM courses
JOIN departments ON courses.department_id = departments.id
JOIN enrollments ON courses.id = enrollments.course_id;
quizDraft.solutionCode:
SELECT courses.title, departments.name, enrollments.term
FROM courses
JOIN departments ON courses.department_id = departments.id
JOIN enrollments ON courses.id = enrollments.course_id;

quizDraft.title: Add Student Details
quizDraft.prompt: Extend the query to include student details, returning the student name and grade level.
quizDraft.hint: Join the students table using the student_id from enrollments.

help.concept: Completing a multi-table join path to include all necessary details.
help.hint_1: The students table connects to enrollments via student_id.
help.hint_2: Select student name and grade level in the final output.

quizDraft.starterCode:
SELECT courses.title, departments.name, students.name, students.grade_level, enrollments.term
FROM courses
JOIN departments ON courses.department_id = departments.id
JOIN enrollments ON courses.id = enrollments.course_id
JOIN students ON enrollments.student_id = students.id;
quizDraft.solutionCode:
SELECT courses.title, departments.name, students.name, students.grade_level, enrollments.term
FROM courses
JOIN departments ON courses.department_id = departments.id
JOIN enrollments ON courses.id = enrollments.course_id
JOIN students ON enrollments.student_id = students.id;

quizDraft.title: Clarify the Directory Output
quizDraft.prompt: Use exact aliases for department, course, student, grade_level, and term without changing the row grain.
quizDraft.hint: Use the AS keyword to create aliases for the selected columns.

help.concept: Using aliases to clarify column names in the output.
help.hint_1: Aliases make the output more readable and understandable.
help.hint_2: Use AS to rename columns in the SELECT statement.

quizDraft.starterCode:
SELECT courses.title AS course, departments.name AS department, students.name AS student, students.grade_level AS grade_level, enrollments.term AS term
FROM courses
JOIN departments ON courses.department_id = departments.id
JOIN enrollments ON courses.id = enrollments.course_id
JOIN students ON enrollments.student_id = students.id;
quizDraft.solutionCode:
SELECT courses.title AS course, departments.name AS department, students.name AS student, students.grade_level AS grade_level, enrollments.term AS term
FROM courses
JOIN departments ON courses.department_id = departments.id
JOIN enrollments ON courses.id = enrollments.course_id
JOIN students ON enrollments.student_id = students.id;

quizDraft.title: Sort the Directory for Review
quizDraft.prompt: Order the results by department, course, and student ascending with deterministic output.
quizDraft.hint: Use ORDER BY to sort the results based on the specified columns.

help.concept: Sorting results to ensure a consistent and readable output.
help.hint_1: ORDER BY allows sorting by multiple columns in sequence.
help.hint_2: Ensure the order is department, then course, then student.

quizDraft.starterCode:
SELECT courses.title AS course, departments.name AS department, students.name AS student, students.grade_level AS grade_level, enrollments.term AS term
FROM courses
JOIN departments ON courses.department_id = departments.id
JOIN enrollments ON courses.id = enrollments.course_id
JOIN students ON enrollments.student_id = students.id
ORDER BY department, course, student;
quizDraft.solutionCode:
SELECT courses.title AS course, departments.name AS department, students.name AS student, students.grade_level AS grade_level, enrollments.term AS term
FROM courses
JOIN departments ON courses.department_id = departments.id
JOIN enrollments ON courses.id = enrollments.course_id
JOIN students ON enrollments.student_id = students.id
ORDER BY department, course, student;
